behavior: An agile, vocal seed-eater that often hangs from seedheads and uses its pointed bill to extract seeds. Usually forages in pairs or small groups while breeding, forms larger flocks outside the breeding season, and flies with an undulating motion.
field_marks: Small, compact finch with a long, pointed pale bill and dark eye
Scarlet-red face bordered by a sharply patterned black-and-white head
Warm buff-brown back and flanks contrasting with whitish underparts
Black wings crossed by a broad, vivid yellow bar and marked with small white spots
Strongly notched black tail with white markings
habitat: Open and lightly wooded country, including woodland edges, orchards, hedgerows, gardens, parks, meadows, grasslands, and weedy fields rich in seed-bearing plants.
